name: bump-dependency
tagline: Step-by-step workflow to bump a Python dependency in Home Assistant core, run tests/lint, and open a PR.
verdict: Thorough and safely gated: pinned installs, standard registries, and an explicit stop-for-user-approval step before the PR is raised. Weak spots: uv/prek/gh are relied on without an upfront availability check, re-running against the same target isn't addressed, and there's no versioning signal.

highlights.summary: Re-running for the same package/version would hit 'git checkout -b' on an existing branch name; no idempotency check, though it would fail loudly rather than corrupt state.
Relies on uv, prek, and an authenticated gh CLI throughout with no upfront requirements/availability check.
No version field, changelog, or dated revision notes anywhere in the file.
